1193	func (h *HugoSites) fileEventsFilter(events []fsnotify.Event) []fsnotify.Event {
1194		seen := make(map[fsnotify.Event]bool)
1195	
1196		n := 0
1197		for _, ev := range events {
1198			// Avoid processing the same event twice.
1199			if seen[ev] {
1200				continue
1201			}
1202			seen[ev] = true
1203	
1204			if h.SourceSpec.IgnoreFile(ev.Name) {
1205				continue
1206			}
1207	
1208			if runtime.GOOS == "darwin" { // When a file system is HFS+, its filepath is in NFD form.
1209				ev.Name = norm.NFC.String(ev.Name)
1210			}
1211	
1212			events[n] = ev
1213			n++
1214		}
1215		events = events[:n]
1216	
1217		eventOrdinal := func(e fsnotify.Event) int {
1218			// Pull the structural changes to the top.
1219			if e.Op.Has(fsnotify.Create) {
1220				return 1
1221			}
1222			if e.Op.Has(fsnotify.Remove) {
1223				return 2
1224			}
1225			if e.Op.Has(fsnotify.Rename) {
1226				return 3
1227			}
1228			if e.Op.Has(fsnotify.Write) {
1229				return 4
1230			}
1231			return 5
1232		}
1233	
1234		sort.Slice(events, func(i, j int) bool {
1235			// First sort by event type.
1236			if eventOrdinal(events[i]) != eventOrdinal(events[j]) {
1237				return eventOrdinal(events[i]) < eventOrdinal(events[j])
1238			}
1239			// Then sort by name.
1240			return events[i].Name < events[j].Name
1241		})
1242	
1243		return events
1244	}
1245	
1246	type fileEventInfo struct {
1247		fsnotify.Event
1248		fi           os.FileInfo
1249		added        bool
1250		removed      bool
1251		isChangedDir bool
1252	}
1253	
1254	func (h *HugoSites) fileEventsApplyInfo(events []fsnotify.Event) []fileEventInfo {
1255		var infos []fileEventInfo
1256		for _, ev := range events {
1257			removed := false
1258			added := false
1259	
1260			if ev.Op&fsnotify.Remove == fsnotify.Remove {
1261				removed = true
1262			}
1263	
1264			fi, statErr := h.Fs.Source.Stat(ev.Name)
1265	
1266			// Some editors (Vim) sometimes issue only a Rename operation when writing an existing file
1267			// Sometimes a rename operation means that file has been renamed other times it means
1268			// it's been updated.
1269			if ev.Op.Has(fsnotify.Rename) {
1270				// If the file is still on disk, it's only been updated, if it's not, it's been moved
1271				if statErr != nil {
1272					removed = true
1273				}
1274			}
1275			if ev.Op.Has(fsnotify.Create) {
1276				added = true
1277			}
1278	
1279			isChangedDir := statErr == nil && fi.IsDir()
1280	
1281			infos = append(infos, fileEventInfo{
1282				Event:        ev,
1283				fi:           fi,
1284				added:        added,
1285				removed:      removed,
1286				isChangedDir: isChangedDir,
1287			})
1288		}
1289	
1290		n := 0
1291	
1292		for _, ev := range infos {
1293			// Remove any directories that's also represented by a file.
1294			keep := true
1295			if ev.isChangedDir {
1296				for _, ev2 := range infos {
1297					if ev2.fi != nil && !ev2.fi.IsDir() && filepath.Dir(ev2.Name) == ev.Name {
1298						keep = false
1299						break
1300					}
1301				}
1302			}
1303			if keep {
1304				infos[n] = ev
1305				n++
1306			}
1307		}
1308		infos = infos[:n]
1309	
1310		return infos
1311	}
1312	
1313	func (h *HugoSites) fileEventsTrim(events []fsnotify.Event) []fsnotify.Event {
1314		seen := make(map[string]bool)
1315		n := 0
1316		for _, ev := range events {
1317			if seen[ev.Name] {
1318				continue
1319			}
1320			seen[ev.Name] = true
1321			events[n] = ev
1322			n++
1323		}
1324		return events
1325	}
1326	
1327	func (h *HugoSites) fileEventsContentPaths(p []pathChange) []pathChange {
1328		var bundles []pathChange
1329		var dirs []pathChange
1330		var regular []pathChange
1331	
1332		var others []pathChange
1333		for _, p := range p {
1334			if p.isDir {
1335				dirs = append(dirs, p)
1336			} else {
1337				others = append(others, p)
1338			}
1339		}
1340	
1341		// Remove all files below dir.
1342		if len(dirs) > 0 {
1343			n := 0
1344			for _, o := range others {
1345				keep := true
1346				for _, d := range dirs {
1347					if strings.HasPrefix(o.p.Path(), d.p.Path()+"/") {
1348						keep = false
1349						break
1350					}
1351				}
1352				if keep {
1353					others[n] = o
1354					n++
1355				}
1356			}
1357			others = others[:n]
1358		}
1359	
1360		for _, p := range others {
1361			if p.p.IsBundle() {
1362				bundles = append(bundles, p)
1363			} else {
1364				regular = append(regular, p)
1365			}
1366		}
1367	
1368		// Remove any files below leaf bundles.
1369		// Remove any files in the same folder as branch bundles.
1370		var keepers []pathChange
1371	
1372		for _, o := range regular {
1373			keep := true
1374			for _, b := range bundles {
1375				prefix := b.p.Base() + "/"
1376				if b.p.IsLeafBundle() && strings.HasPrefix(o.p.Path(), prefix) {
1377					keep = false
1378					break
1379				} else if b.p.IsBranchBundle() && o.p.Dir() == b.p.Dir() {
1380					keep = false
1381					break
1382				}
1383			}
1384	
1385			if keep {
1386				keepers = append(keepers, o)
1387			}
1388		}
1389	
1390		keepers = append(dirs, keepers...)
1391		keepers = append(bundles, keepers...)
1392	
1393		return keepers
1394	}

1396	// SitemapAbsURL is a convenience method giving the absolute URL to the sitemap.
1397	func (s *Site) SitemapAbsURL() string {
1398		base := ""
1399		if s.Conf.IsMultilingual() || s.Conf.DefaultContentLanguageInSubdir() {
1400			base = s.Language().Lang
1401		}
1402		p := s.AbsURL(base, false)
1403		if !strings.HasSuffix(p, "/") {
1404			p += "/"
1405		}
1406		p += s.conf.Sitemap.Filename
1407		return p
1408	}

1410	func (s *Site) createNodeMenuEntryURL(in string) string {
1411		if !strings.HasPrefix(in, "/") {
1412			return in
1413		}
1414		// make it match the nodes
1415		menuEntryURL := in
1416		menuEntryURL = s.PathSpec.URLize(menuEntryURL)
1417		if !s.conf.CanonifyURLs {
1418			menuEntryURL = paths.AddContextRoot(s.PathSpec.Cfg.BaseURL().String(), menuEntryURL)
1419		}
1420		return menuEntryURL
1421	}
1422	
1423	func (s *Site) assembleMenus() (navigation.Menus, error) {
1424		menus := make(navigation.Menus)
1425	
1426		type twoD struct {
1427			MenuName, EntryName string
1428		}
1429		flat := map[twoD]*navigation.MenuEntry{}
1430		children := map[twoD]navigation.Menu{}
1431	
1432		// add menu entries from config to flat hash
1433		for name, menu := range s.conf.Menus.Config {
1434			for _, x := range menu {
1435				// Shallow copy the menu entry, so we can set the URL and page without modifying the config.
1436				c := *x
1437				me := &c
1438	
1439				if me.PageRef != "" {
1440					// Try to resolve the page.
1441					me.Page, _ = s.getPage(nil, me.PageRef)
1442				}
1443	
1444				// If page is still nill, we must make sure that we have a URL that considers baseURL etc.
1445				if types.IsNil(me.Page) {
1446					me.ConfiguredURL = s.createNodeMenuEntryURL(me.MenuConfig.URL)
1447				} else {
1448					navigation.SetPageValues(me, me.Page)
1449				}
1450	
1451				flat[twoD{name, me.KeyName()}] = me
1452			}
1453		}
1454	
1455		sectionPagesMenu := s.conf.SectionPagesMenu
1456	
1457		if sectionPagesMenu != "" {
1458			if err := s.pageMap.forEachPage(pagePredicates.ShouldListGlobal, func(p *pageState) (bool, error) {
1459				if p.Kind() != kinds.KindSection || !p.m.shouldBeCheckedForMenuDefinitions() {
1460					return false, nil
1461				}
1462	
1463				// The section pages menus are attached to the top level section.
1464				id := p.Section()
1465				if id == "" {
1466					id = "/"
1467				}
1468	
1469				if _, ok := flat[twoD{sectionPagesMenu, id}]; ok {
1470					return false, nil
1471				}
1472				me := navigation.MenuEntry{
1473					MenuConfig: navigation.MenuConfig{
1474						Identifier: id,
1475						Name:       p.LinkTitle(),
1476						Weight:     p.Weight(),
1477					},
1478					Page: p,
1479				}
1480	
1481				navigation.SetPageValues(&me, p)
1482				flat[twoD{sectionPagesMenu, me.KeyName()}] = &me
1483				return false, nil
1484			}); err != nil {
1485				return nil, err
1486			}
1487		}
1488	
1489		// Add menu entries provided by pages
1490		if err := s.pageMap.forEachPage(pagePredicates.ShouldListGlobal, func(p *pageState) (bool, error) {
1491			for name, me := range p.pageMenus.menus() {
1492				if _, ok := flat[twoD{name, me.KeyName()}]; ok {
1493					err := p.wrapError(fmt.Errorf("duplicate menu entry with identifier %q in menu %q", me.KeyName(), name))
1494					s.Log.Warnln(err)
1495					continue
1496				}
1497				flat[twoD{name, me.KeyName()}] = me
1498			}
1499			return false, nil
1500		}); err != nil {
1501			return nil, err
1502		}
1503	
1504		// Create Children Menus First
1505		for _, e := range flat {
1506			if e.Parent != "" {
1507				children[twoD{e.Menu, e.Parent}] = children[twoD{e.Menu, e.Parent}].Add(e)
1508			}
1509		}
1510	
1511		// Placing Children in Parents (in flat)
1512		for p, childmenu := range children {
1513			_, ok := flat[twoD{p.MenuName, p.EntryName}]
1514			if !ok {
1515				// if parent does not exist, create one without a URL
1516				flat[twoD{p.MenuName, p.EntryName}] = &navigation.MenuEntry{
1517					MenuConfig: navigation.MenuConfig{
1518						Name: p.EntryName,
1519					},
1520				}
1521			}
1522			flat[twoD{p.MenuName, p.EntryName}].Children = childmenu
1523		}
1524	
1525		// Assembling Top Level of Tree
1526		for menu, e := range flat {
1527			if e.Parent == "" {
1528				_, ok := menus[menu.MenuName]
1529				if !ok {
1530					menus[menu.MenuName] = navigation.Menu{}
1531				}
1532				menus[menu.MenuName] = menus[menu.MenuName].Add(e)
1533			}
1534		}
1535	
1536		return menus, nil
1537	}

1590	func (s *Site) errorCollator(results <-chan error, errs chan<- error) {
1591		var errors []error
1592		defer func() {
1593			errs <- s.h.filterAndJoinErrors(errors)
1594			close(errs)
1595		}()
1596		const maxErrors = 10
1597		for e := range results {
1598			errors = append(errors, e)
1599			if len(errors) >= maxErrors {
1600				s.h.Stop()
1601				break
1602			}
1603		}
1604	}

1606	// GetPage looks up a page of a given type for the given ref.
1607	// In Hugo <= 0.44 you had to add Page Kind (section, home) etc. as the first
1608	// argument and then either a unix styled path (with or without a leading slash))
1609	// or path elements separated.
1610	// When we now remove the Kind from this API, we need to make the transition as painless
1611	// as possible for existing sites. Most sites will use {{ .Site.GetPage "section" "my/section" }},
1612	// i.e. 2 arguments, so we test for that.
1613	func (s *Site) GetPage(ref ...string) (page.Page, error) {
1614		s.CheckReady()
1615		p, err := s.getPageForRefs(ref...)
1616	
1617		if p == nil {
1618			// The nil struct has meaning in some situations, mostly to avoid breaking
1619			// existing sites doing $nilpage.IsDescendant($p), which will always return
1620			// false.
1621			p = page.NilPage
1622		}
1623	
1624		return p, err
1625	}

1627	func (s *Site) absURLPath(targetPath string) string {
1628		var path string
1629		if s.conf.RelativeURLs {
1630			path = helpers.GetDottedRelativePath(targetPath)
1631		} else {
1632			url := s.PathSpec.Cfg.BaseURL().String()
1633			if !strings.HasSuffix(url, "/") {
1634				url += "/"
1635			}
1636			path = url
1637		}
1638	
1639		return path
1640	}
1641	
1642	const (
1643		pageDependencyScopeDefault int = iota
1644		pageDependencyScopeGlobal
1645	)
1646	
1647	func (s *Site) renderAndWritePage(statCounter *uint64, targetPath string, p *pageState, d any, templ *tplimpl.TemplInfo) error {
1648		s.h.onPageRender()
1649		renderBuffer := bp.GetBuffer()
1650		defer bp.PutBuffer(renderBuffer)
1651	
1652		of := p.outputFormat()
1653		p.incrRenderState()
1654	
1655		ctx := s.TemplateStore.PrepareTopLevelRenderCtx(context.Background(), p)
1656		ctx = tpl.Context.DependencyManagerScopedProvider.Set(ctx, p)
1657	
1658		if err := s.renderForTemplate(ctx, p.Kind(), of.Name, d, renderBuffer, templ); err != nil {
1659			return err
1660		}
1661	
1662		if renderBuffer.Len() == 0 {
1663			return nil
1664		}
1665	
1666		isHTML := of.IsHTML
1667		isRSS := of.Name == "rss"
1668	
1669		pd := publisher.Descriptor{
1670			Src:          renderBuffer,
1671			TargetPath:   targetPath,
1672			StatCounter:  statCounter,
1673			OutputFormat: p.outputFormat(),
1674		}
1675	
1676		if isRSS {
1677			// Always canonify URLs in RSS
1678			pd.AbsURLPath = s.absURLPath(targetPath)
1679		} else if isHTML {
1680			if s.conf.RelativeURLs || s.conf.CanonifyURLs {
1681				pd.AbsURLPath = s.absURLPath(targetPath)
1682			}
1683	
1684			if s.watching() && s.conf.Internal.Running && !s.conf.DisableLiveReload {
1685				pd.LiveReloadBaseURL = s.Conf.BaseURLLiveReload().URL()
1686			}
1687	
1688			// For performance reasons we only inject the Hugo generator tag on the home page.
1689			if p.IsHome() {
1690				pd.AddHugoGeneratorTag = !s.conf.DisableHugoGeneratorInject
1691			}
1692	
1693		}
1694	
1695		return s.publisher.Publish(pd)
1696	}
1697	
1698	var infoOnMissingLayout = map[string]bool{
1699		// The 404 layout is very much optional in Hugo, but we do look for it.
1700		"404": true,
1701	}
1702	
1703	// hookRendererTemplate is the canonical implementation of all hooks.ITEMRenderer,
1704	// where ITEM is the thing being hooked.
1705	type hookRendererTemplate struct {
1706		templateHandler *tplimpl.TemplateStore
1707		templ           *tplimpl.TemplInfo
1708		resolvePosition func(renderContext any, pos int) text.Position
1709	}
1710	
1711	func (hr hookRendererTemplate) RenderLink(cctx context.Context, w io.Writer, ctx hooks.LinkContext) error {
1712		return hr.templateHandler.ExecuteWithContext(cctx, hr.templ, w, ctx)
1713	}
1714	
1715	func (hr hookRendererTemplate) RenderHeading(cctx context.Context, w io.Writer, ctx hooks.HeadingContext) error {
1716		return hr.templateHandler.ExecuteWithContext(cctx, hr.templ, w, ctx)
1717	}
1718	
1719	func (hr hookRendererTemplate) RenderCodeblock(cctx context.Context, w hugio.FlexiWriter, ctx hooks.CodeblockContext) error {
1720		return hr.templateHandler.ExecuteWithContext(cctx, hr.templ, w, ctx)
1721	}
1722	
1723	func (hr hookRendererTemplate) RenderPassthrough(cctx context.Context, w io.Writer, ctx hooks.PassthroughContext) error {
1724		return hr.templateHandler.ExecuteWithContext(cctx, hr.templ, w, ctx)
1725	}
1726	
1727	func (hr hookRendererTemplate) RenderBlockquote(cctx context.Context, w hugio.FlexiWriter, ctx hooks.BlockquoteContext) error {
1728		return hr.templateHandler.ExecuteWithContext(cctx, hr.templ, w, ctx)
1729	}
1730	
1731	func (hr hookRendererTemplate) RenderTable(cctx context.Context, w hugio.FlexiWriter, ctx hooks.TableContext) error {
1732		return hr.templateHandler.ExecuteWithContext(cctx, hr.templ, w, ctx)
1733	}
1734	
1735	func (hr hookRendererTemplate) ResolvePosition(renderContext any, pos int) text.Position {
1736		return hr.resolvePosition(renderContext, pos)
1737	}
1738	
1739	func (hr hookRendererTemplate) IsDefaultCodeBlockRenderer() bool {
1740		return false
1741	}
1742	
1743	func (s *Site) renderForTemplate(ctx context.Context, name, outputFormat string, d any, w io.Writer, templ *tplimpl.TemplInfo) (err error) {
1744		if templ == nil {
1745			s.logMissingLayout(name, "", "", outputFormat)
1746			return nil
1747		}
1748	
1749		if ctx == nil {
1750			panic("nil context")
1751		}
1752	
1753		if err = s.GetTemplateStore().ExecuteWithContext(ctx, templ, w, d); err != nil {
1754			filename := name
1755			if p, ok := d.(*pageState); ok {
1756				filename = p.String()
1757			}
1758			return fmt.Errorf("render of %q failed: %w", filename, err)
1759		}
1760		return
1761	}
1762	
1763	func (s *Site) shouldBuild(p page.Page) bool {
1764		if !s.conf.IsKindEnabled(p.Kind()) {
1765			return false
1766		}
1767		return shouldBuild(s.Conf.BuildFuture(), s.Conf.BuildExpired(),
1768			s.Conf.BuildDrafts(), p.Draft(), p.PublishDate(), p.ExpiryDate())
1769	}
1770	
1771	func shouldBuild(buildFuture bool, buildExpired bool, buildDrafts bool, Draft bool,
1772		publishDate time.Time, expiryDate time.Time,
1773	) bool {
1774		if !(buildDrafts || !Draft) {
1775			return false
1776		}
1777		hnow := htime.Now()
1778		if !buildFuture && !publishDate.IsZero() && publishDate.After(hnow) {
1779			return false
1780		}
1781		if !buildExpired && !expiryDate.IsZero() && expiryDate.Before(hnow) {
1782			return false
1783		}
1784		return true
1785	}
1786	
1787	func (s *Site) render(ctx *siteRenderContext) (err error) {
1788		if err := page.Clear(); err != nil {
1789			return err
1790		}
1791	
1792		if err = s.renderPages(ctx); err != nil {
1793			return
1794		}
1795	
1796		if !ctx.shouldRenderStandalonePage("") {
1797			return
1798		}
1799	
1800		return
1801	}
1802	
1803	func (s *Site) String() string {
1804		if s == nil {
1805			return "Site (nil)"
1806		}
1807	
1808		return fmt.Sprintf("Site %v: %s", s.siteVector, s.resolveDimensionNames())
1809	}

54	// Deps holds dependencies used by many.
55	// There will be normally only one instance of deps in play
56	// at a given time, i.e. one per Site built.
57	type Deps struct {
58		// The logger to use.
59		Log loggers.Logger `json:"-"`
60	
61		ExecHelper *hexec.Exec
62	
63		// The file systems to use.
64		Fs *hugofs.Fs `json:"-"`
65	
66		// The PathSpec to use
67		*helpers.PathSpec `json:"-"`
68	
69		// The ContentSpec to use
70		*helpers.ContentSpec `json:"-"`
71	
72		// The SourceSpec to use
73		SourceSpec *source.SourceSpec `json:"-"`
74	
75		// The Resource Spec to use
76		ResourceSpec *resources.Spec
77	
78		// The configuration to use
79		Conf config.AllProvider `json:"-"`
80	
81		// The memory cache to use.
82		MemCache *dynacache.Cache
83	
84		// The translation func to use
85		Translate func(ctx context.Context, translationID string, templateData any) string `json:"-"`
86	
87		// The site building.
88		Site page.Site
89	
90		TemplateStore *tplimpl.TemplateStore
91	
92		// Used in tests
93		OverloadedTemplateFuncs map[string]any
94	
95		TranslationProvider ResourceProvider
96	
97		Metrics metrics.Provider
98	
99		// BuildStartListeners will be notified before a build starts.
100		BuildStartListeners *Listeners[any]
101	
102		// BuildEndListeners will be notified after a build finishes.
103		BuildEndListeners *Listeners[any]
104	
105		// OnChangeListeners will be notified when something changes.
106		OnChangeListeners *Listeners[identity.Identity]
107	
108		// Resources that gets closed when the build is done or the server shuts down.
109		BuildClosers *types.Closers
110	
111		// This is common/global for all sites.
112		BuildState *BuildState
113	
114		// Misc counters.
115		Counters *Counters
116	
117		// Holds RPC dispatchers for Katex etc.
118		// TODO(bep) rethink this re. a plugin setup, but this will have to do for now.
119		WasmDispatchers *warpc.Dispatchers
120	
121		// The JS batcher client.
122		JSBatcherClient js.BatcherClient
123	
124		isClosed bool
125	
126		*globalErrHandler
127	}

129	func (d Deps) Clone(s page.Site, conf config.AllProvider) (*Deps, error) {
130		d.Conf = conf
131		d.Site = s
132		d.ExecHelper = nil
133		d.ContentSpec = nil
134	
135		if err := d.Init(); err != nil {
136			return nil, err
137		}
138	
139		return &d, nil
140	}
141	
142	func (d *Deps) GetTemplateStore() *tplimpl.TemplateStore {
143		return d.TemplateStore
144	}
145	
146	func (d *Deps) Init() error {
147		if d.Conf == nil {
148			panic("conf is nil")
149		}
150	
151		if d.Fs == nil {
152			// For tests.
153			d.Fs = hugofs.NewFrom(afero.NewMemMapFs(), d.Conf.BaseConfig())
154		}
155	
156		if d.Log == nil {
157			d.Log = loggers.NewDefault()
158		}
159	
160		if d.globalErrHandler == nil {
161			d.globalErrHandler = &globalErrHandler{
162				logger: d.Log,
163			}
164		}
165		if d.BuildState == nil {
166			d.BuildState = &BuildState{}
167		}
168		if d.Counters == nil {
169			d.Counters = &Counters{}
170		}
171		if d.BuildState.DeferredExecutions == nil {
172			if d.BuildState.DeferredExecutionsGroupedByRenderingContext == nil {
173				d.BuildState.DeferredExecutionsGroupedByRenderingContext = make(map[tpl.RenderingContext]*DeferredExecutions)
174			}
175			d.BuildState.DeferredExecutions = &DeferredExecutions{
176				Executions:              hmaps.NewCache[string, *tpl.DeferredExecution](),
177				FilenamesWithPostPrefix: hmaps.NewCache[string, bool](),
178			}
179		}
180	
181		if d.BuildStartListeners == nil {
182			d.BuildStartListeners = &Listeners[any]{}
183		}
184	
185		if d.BuildEndListeners == nil {
186			d.BuildEndListeners = &Listeners[any]{}
187		}
188	
189		if d.BuildClosers == nil {
190			d.BuildClosers = &types.Closers{}
191		}
192	
193		if d.OnChangeListeners == nil {
194			d.OnChangeListeners = &Listeners[identity.Identity]{}
195		}
196	
197		if d.Metrics == nil && d.Conf.TemplateMetrics() {
198			d.Metrics = metrics.NewProvider(d.Conf.TemplateMetricsHints())
199		}
200	
201		if d.ExecHelper == nil {
202			d.ExecHelper = hexec.New(d.Conf.GetConfigSection("security").(security.Config), d.Conf.WorkingDir(), d.Log)
203		}
204	
205		if d.MemCache == nil {
206			d.MemCache = dynacache.New(dynacache.Options{Watching: d.Conf.Watching(), Log: d.Log})
207		}
208	
209		if d.PathSpec == nil {
210			hashBytesReceiverFunc := func(name string, match []byte) {
211				s := string(match)
212				switch s {
213				case postpub.PostProcessPrefix:
214					d.BuildState.AddFilenameWithPostPrefix(name)
215				case tpl.HugoDeferredTemplatePrefix:
216					d.BuildState.DeferredExecutions.FilenamesWithPostPrefix.Set(name, true)
217				}
218			}
219	
220			// Skip binary files.
221			mediaTypes := d.Conf.GetConfigSection("mediaTypes").(media.Types)
222			hashBytesShouldCheck := func(name string) bool {
223				ext := strings.TrimPrefix(filepath.Ext(name), ".")
224				return mediaTypes.IsTextSuffix(ext)
225			}
226			d.Fs.PublishDir = hugofs.NewHasBytesReceiver(
227				d.Fs.PublishDir,
228				hashBytesShouldCheck,
229				hashBytesReceiverFunc,
230				[]byte(tpl.HugoDeferredTemplatePrefix),
231				[]byte(postpub.PostProcessPrefix))
232	
233			pathSpec, err := helpers.NewPathSpec(d.Fs, d.Conf, d.Log, nil)
234			if err != nil {
235				return err
236			}
237			d.PathSpec = pathSpec
238		} else {
239			var err error
240			d.PathSpec, err = helpers.NewPathSpec(d.Fs, d.Conf, d.Log, d.PathSpec.BaseFs)
241			if err != nil {
242				return err
243			}
244		}
245	
246		d.ExecHelper.SetNodeReadPaths(d.BaseFs.Assets.RealPaths(""))
247	
248		if d.ContentSpec == nil {
249			contentSpec, err := helpers.NewContentSpec(d.Conf, d.Log, d.Content.Fs, d.ExecHelper)
250			if err != nil {
251				return err
252			}
253			d.ContentSpec = contentSpec
254		}
255	
256		if d.SourceSpec == nil {
257			d.SourceSpec = source.NewSourceSpec(d.PathSpec, nil, d.Fs.Source)
258		}
259	
260		var common *resources.SpecCommon
261		if d.ResourceSpec != nil {
262			common = d.ResourceSpec.SpecCommon
263		}
264	
265		d.Cfg.BaseConfig()
266	
267		fileCaches := d.Cfg.FileCaches().(filecache.Caches)
268		fileCaches.SetResourceFs(d.BaseFs.ResourcesCache)
269	
270		resourceSpec, err := resources.NewSpec(d.PathSpec, common, d.WasmDispatchers, fileCaches, d.MemCache, d.BuildState, d.Log, d, d.ExecHelper, d.BuildClosers, d.BuildState)
271		if err != nil {
272			return fmt.Errorf("failed to create resource spec: %w", err)
273		}
274		d.ResourceSpec = resourceSpec
275	
276		return nil
277	}

279	// TODO(bep) rework this to get it in line with how we manage templates.
280	func (d *Deps) Compile(prototype *Deps) error {
281		var err error
282		if prototype == nil {
283	
284			if err = d.TranslationProvider.NewResource(d); err != nil {
285				return err
286			}
287			return nil
288		}
289	
290		if err = d.TranslationProvider.CloneResource(d, prototype); err != nil {
291			return err
292		}
293	
294		return nil
295	}

297	// MkdirTemp returns a temporary directory path that will be cleaned up on exit.
298	func (d Deps) MkdirTemp(pattern string) (string, error) {
299		filename, err := os.MkdirTemp("", pattern)
300		if err != nil {
301			return "", err
302		}
303		d.BuildClosers.Add(
304			types.CloserFunc(
305				func() error {
306					return os.RemoveAll(filename)
307				},
308			),
309		)
310	
311		return filename, nil
312	}

314	type globalErrHandler struct {
315		logger loggers.Logger
316	
317		// Channel for some "hard to get to" build errors
318		buildErrors chan error
319		// Used to signal that the build is done.
320		quit chan struct{}
321	}
322	
323	// SendError sends the error on a channel to be handled later.
324	// This can be used in situations where returning and aborting the current
325	// operation isn't practical.
326	func (e *globalErrHandler) SendError(err error) {
327		if e.buildErrors != nil {
328			select {
329			case <-e.quit:
330			case e.buildErrors <- err:
331			default:
332			}
333			return
334		}
335		e.logger.Errorln(err)
336	}
337	
338	func (e *globalErrHandler) StartErrorCollector() chan error {
339		e.quit = make(chan struct{})
340		e.buildErrors = make(chan error, 10)
341		return e.buildErrors
342	}
343	
344	func (e *globalErrHandler) StopErrorCollector() {
345		if e.buildErrors != nil {
346			close(e.quit)
347			close(e.buildErrors)
348		}
349	}
350	
351	// Listeners represents an event listener.
352	type Listeners[T any] struct {
353		sync.Mutex
354	
355		// A list of funcs to be notified about an event.
356		// If the return value is true, the listener will be removed.
357		listeners []func(...T) bool
358	}
359	
360	// Add adds a function to a Listeners instance.
361	func (b *Listeners[T]) Add(f func(...T) bool) {
362		if b == nil {
363			return
364		}
365		b.Lock()
366		defer b.Unlock()
367		b.listeners = append(b.listeners, f)
368	}
369	
370	// Notify executes all listener functions.
371	func (b *Listeners[T]) Notify(vs ...T) {
372		b.Lock()
373		defer b.Unlock()
374		temp := b.listeners[:0]
375		for _, notify := range b.listeners {
376			if !notify(vs...) {
377				temp = append(temp, notify)
378			}
379		}
380		b.listeners = temp
381	}
382	
383	// ResourceProvider is used to create and refresh, and clone resources needed.
384	type ResourceProvider interface {
385		NewResource(dst *Deps) error
386		CloneResource(dst, src *Deps) error
387	}
388	
389	func (d *Deps) Close() error {
390		if d.isClosed {
391			return nil
392		}
393		d.isClosed = true
394	
395		if d.MemCache != nil {
396			d.MemCache.Stop()
397		}
398		if d.WasmDispatchers != nil {
399			d.WasmDispatchers.Close()
400		}
401		return d.BuildClosers.Close()
402	}
403	
404	// DepsCfg contains configuration options that can be used to configure Hugo
405	// on a global level, i.e. logging etc.
406	// Nil values will be given default values.
407	type DepsCfg struct {
408		// The logger to use. Only set in some tests.
409		// TODO(bep) get rid of this.
410		TestLogger loggers.Logger
411	
412		// The logging level to use.
413		LogLevel logg.Level
414	
415		// Logging output.
416		StdErr io.Writer
417	
418		// The console output.
419		StdOut io.Writer
420	
421		// The file systems to use
422		Fs *hugofs.Fs
423	
424		// The Site in use
425		Site page.Site
426	
427		Configs *allconfig.Configs
428	
429		// Template handling.
430		TemplateProvider ResourceProvider
431	
432		// i18n handling.
433		TranslationProvider ResourceProvider
434	
435		// Build triggered by the IntegrationTest framework.
436		IsIntegrationTest bool
437	
438		// TestCfg holds configuration used only in tests.
439		// It is a programming error to set this when IsIntegrationTest is not set,
440		// and doing so will panic.
441		TestCfg TestConfig
442	
443		// ChangesFromBuild for changes passed back to the server/watch process.
444		ChangesFromBuild chan []identity.Identity
445	}
446	
447	// TestConfig holds configuration used only in tests.
448	// See DepsCfg.TestCfg.
449	type TestConfig struct {
450		// WarpcMemory, if set, overrides the memory limit in MiB for the WASM based
451		// image processors (WebP and AVIF). Used to provoke memory allocation failures.
452		WarpcMemory int
453	}
454	
455	// IsZero reports whether c holds no test configuration.
456	func (c TestConfig) IsZero() bool {
457		return c == TestConfig{}
458	}
459	
460	// BuildState are state used during a build.
461	type BuildState struct {
462		counter uint64
463	
464		// Tracks invocations of the Build method.
465		BuildCounter atomic.Uint64
466	
467		mu sync.Mutex // protects state below.
468	
469		OnSignalRebuild func(ids ...identity.Identity)
470	
471		// A set of filenames in /public that
472		// contains a post-processing prefix.
473		filenamesWithPostPrefix map[string]bool
474	
475		DeferredExecutions *DeferredExecutions
476	
477		// Deferred executions grouped by rendering context.
478		DeferredExecutionsGroupedByRenderingContext map[tpl.RenderingContext]*DeferredExecutions
479	}
480	
481	// Misc counters.
482	type Counters struct {
483		// Counter for the math.Counter function.
484		MathCounter atomic.Uint64
485	}
486	
487	type DeferredExecutions struct {
488		// A set of filenames in /public that
489		// contains a post-processing prefix.
490		FilenamesWithPostPrefix *hmaps.Cache[string, bool]
491	
492		// Maps a placeholder to a deferred execution.
493		Executions *hmaps.Cache[string, *tpl.DeferredExecution]
494	}
495	
496	var _ identity.SignalRebuilder = (*BuildState)(nil)
497	
498	// IsRebuild reports whether this is a rebuild.
499	func (b *BuildState) IsRebuild() bool {
500		return b.BuildCounter.Load() > 0
501	}
502	
503	// StartStageRender will be called before a stage is rendered.
504	func (b *BuildState) StartStageRender(stage tpl.RenderingContext) {
505	}
506	
507	// StopStageRender will be called after a stage is rendered.
508	func (b *BuildState) StopStageRender(stage tpl.RenderingContext) {
509		b.DeferredExecutionsGroupedByRenderingContext[stage] = b.DeferredExecutions
510		b.DeferredExecutions = &DeferredExecutions{
511			Executions:              hmaps.NewCache[string, *tpl.DeferredExecution](),
512			FilenamesWithPostPrefix: hmaps.NewCache[string, bool](),
513		}
514	}
515	
516	func (b *BuildState) SignalRebuild(ids ...identity.Identity) {
517		b.OnSignalRebuild(ids...)
518	}
519	
520	func (b *BuildState) AddFilenameWithPostPrefix(filename string) {
521		b.mu.Lock()
522		defer b.mu.Unlock()
523		if b.filenamesWithPostPrefix == nil {
524			b.filenamesWithPostPrefix = make(map[string]bool)
525		}
526		b.filenamesWithPostPrefix[filename] = true
527	}
528	
529	func (b *BuildState) GetFilenamesWithPostPrefix() []string {
530		b.mu.Lock()
531		defer b.mu.Unlock()
532		var filenames []string
533		for filename := range b.filenamesWithPostPrefix {
534			filenames = append(filenames, filename)
535		}
536		sort.Strings(filenames)
537		return filenames
538	}
539	
540	func (b *BuildState) Incr() int {
541		return int(atomic.AddUint64(&b.counter, uint64(1)))
542	}

31	// ProviderConfig configures a new Provider.

33		Conf      config.AllProvider // Project config
34		ContentFs afero.Fs
35		Logger    loggers.Logger
36		Exec      *hexec.Exec
37		highlight.Highlighter
38	}
39	
40	func (p ProviderConfig) MarkupConfig() markup_config.Config {
41		return p.Conf.GetConfigSection("markup").(markup_config.Config)
42	}
43	
44	// ProviderProvider creates converter providers.
45	type ProviderProvider interface {
46		New(cfg ProviderConfig) (Provider, error)
47	}
48	
49	// Provider creates converters.
50	type Provider interface {
51		New(ctx DocumentContext) (Converter, error)
52		Name() string
53	}
54	
55	// NewProvider creates a new Provider with the given name.
56	func NewProvider(name string, create func(ctx DocumentContext) (Converter, error)) Provider {
57		return newConverter{
58			name:   name,
59			create: create,
60		}
61	}
62	
63	type newConverter struct {
64		name   string
65		create func(ctx DocumentContext) (Converter, error)
66	}
67	
68	func (n newConverter) New(ctx DocumentContext) (Converter, error) {
69		return n.create(ctx)
70	}
71	
72	func (n newConverter) Name() string {
73		return n.name
74	}
75	
76	var NopConverter = new(nopConverter)
77	
78	type nopConverter int
79	
80	func (nopConverter) Convert(ctx RenderContext) (ResultRender, error) {
81		return &bytes.Buffer{}, nil
82	}
83	
84	func (nopConverter) Supports(feature identity.Identity) bool {
85		return false
86	}
87	
88	// Converter wraps the Convert method that converts some markup into
89	// another format, e.g. Markdown to HTML.
90	type Converter interface {
91		Convert(ctx RenderContext) (ResultRender, error)
92	}
93	
94	// ParseRenderer is an optional interface.
95	// The Goldmark converter implements this, and this allows us
96	// to extract the ToC without having to render the content.
97	type ParseRenderer interface {
98		Parse(RenderContext) (ResultParse, error)
99		Render(RenderContext, any) (ResultRender, error)
100	}
101	
102	// ResultRender represents the minimum returned from Convert and Render.
103	type ResultRender interface {
104		Bytes() []byte
105	}
106	
107	// ResultParse represents the minimum returned from Parse.
108	type ResultParse interface {
109		Doc() any
110		TableOfContents() *tableofcontents.Fragments
111	}
112	
113	// DocumentInfo holds additional information provided by some converters.
114	type DocumentInfo interface {
115		AnchorSuffix() string
116	}
117	
118	// TableOfContentsProvider provides the content as a ToC structure.
119	type TableOfContentsProvider interface {
120		TableOfContents() *tableofcontents.Fragments
121	}
122	
123	// AnchorNameSanitizer tells how a converter sanitizes anchor names.
124	type AnchorNameSanitizer interface {
125		SanitizeAnchorName(s string) string
126	}
127	
128	// Bytes holds a byte slice and implements the Result interface.
129	type Bytes []byte
130	
131	// Bytes returns itself
132	func (b Bytes) Bytes() []byte {
133		return b
134	}
135	
136	// DocumentContext holds contextual information about the document to convert.
137	type DocumentContext struct {
138		Document       any              // May be nil. Usually a page.Page
139		DocumentLookup func(uint64) any // May be nil.
140		DocumentID     string
141		DocumentName   string
142		Filename       string
143	}
144	
145	// RenderContext holds contextual information about the content to render.
146	type RenderContext struct {
147		// Ctx is the context.Context for the current Page render.
148		Ctx context.Context
149	
150		// Src is the content to render.
151		Src []byte
152	
153		// SourceInfo holds optional information about the source of the content to render.
154		SourceInfo any
155	
156		// Whether to render TableOfContents.
157		RenderTOC bool
158	
159		// GerRenderer provides hook renderers on demand.
160		GetRenderer hooks.GetRendererFunc
161	}

574		}

### Summary

There are **two distinct `Site` types**:

- **`page.Site`** (`resources/page/site.go:37`) — the public interface used almost everywhere outside `hugolib`. It defines the template-visible API. `page.Sites` (`site.go:146`) is a slice of it.
- **`hugolib.Site`** (`hugolib/site.go:103`) — the concrete struct that implements `page.Site`. It lives only inside `hugolib` and is exposed to the rest of the codebase only through the wrapped `page.Site` interface (via `page.WrapSite`).

The two are bridged at `hugolib/site.go:94` (`var _ page.Site = (*Site)(nil)`) and at `Site.Site()` (line 744) which returns `page.WrapSite(s)`.

### Summary

There are **two distinct `Site` types**:

- **`page.Site`** (`resources/page/site.go:37`) — the public interface used almost everywhere outside `hugolib`. It defines the template-visible API. `page.Sites` (`site.go:146`) is a slice of it.
- **`hugolib.Site`** (`hugolib/site.go:103`) — the concrete struct that implements `page.Site`. It lives only inside `hugolib` and is exposed to the rest of the codebase only through the wrapped `page.Site` interface (via `page.WrapSite`).

The two are bridged at `hugolib/site.go:94` (`var _ page.Site = (*Site)(nil)`) and at `Site.Site()` (line 744) which returns `page.WrapSite(s)`.
